commands.review_summary:
The PR changes MS Teams parent-message context summaries to truncate with `truncateUtf16Safe` and adds surrogate-boundary regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +19. Total +22 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main uses `slice(0, 399)` for over-limit parent text, and a parent body with 398 ASCII characters followed by a surrogate-pair emoji hits the split boundary; the PR body also shows terminal proof of the legacy malformed string and fixed output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The code repair is already in this PR; remaining action is maintainer/status-gate validation rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(msteams): keep truncated parent context text well-formed
target.body:
**Summary:** The Teams parent-message summary was truncated with a raw UTF-16 slice, so an emoji at the 400-char boundary became a lone surrogate in the injected `Replying to @sender: ŌĆ”` context event. This uses the shared `truncateUtf16Safe` helper to truncate on a code-point boundary.

## What Problem This Solves

MS Teams thread parent context summarization truncated long parent-message text with raw UTF-16 slicing. When the 400-character parent preview limit landed between the high and low surrogate of an emoji, the generated `Replying to @sender: ...` context could contain a lone surrogate.

Trigger input:

- parent text starts with 398 ASCII characters
- followed by an emoji such as `­¤”×` (`U+1F99E`, encoded as a UTF-16 surrogate pair)
- followed by additional text
- legacy truncation used `slice(0, 399) + "ŌĆ”"` and kept only the high surrogate before the ellipsis

That leaves malformed text in the injected MS Teams parent-context event and can break consumers that require well-formed strings.

## Fix

`extensions/msteams/src/thread-parent-context.ts` now reuses the shared `truncateUtf16Safe` helper from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/text-utility-runtime` when shortening parent-message text.

The fix keeps the existing 400-character parent-context limit and ellipsis behavior, but the truncation boundary is now validated before the ellipsis is appended. If the limit would split a surrogate pair, the helper backs up to the last complete UTF-16 boundary, so the final escaped/injected parent context remains well-formed.

## Evidence

Command run from an isolated temporary worktree on `fix/msteams-parent-truncate`:

```bash
node --import tsx demo.mts
```

Terminal output:

```text
input.length=450
emoji.codePoint=1f99e
BEFORE legacy slice(0, 399) + ellipsis
before.length=400
before.tailCodeUnits=0061 0061 0061 d83e 2026
before.loneSurrogate=true
before.loneSurrogateCodeUnit=d83e
before.encodeURIComponent=URIError
AFTER summarizeParentMessage (real msteams repair path)
after.text=aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaŌĆ”[398 ├Ś'a' elided]ŌĆ”
after.length=399
after.tailCodeUnits=0061 0061 0061 0061 2026
after.loneSurrogate=false
after.endsWithHighSurrogateEllipsis=false
after.encodeURIComponent=aaaaaaaaa%E2%80%A6
```

This demo calls the real exported `summarizeParentMessage` path from `extensions/msteams/src/thread-parent-context.ts`. The legacy reproduction leaves a lone high surrogate (`d83e`) and throws `URIError` during URI encoding; the fixed path returns well-formed text with `loneSurrogate=false`.

## Tests

`extensions/msteams/src/thread-parent-context.test.ts` adds focused Vitest regression coverage for surrogate-boundary truncation in MS Teams parent-context summaries.

The test constructs the same boundary shape, verifies the summarized text does not match the unpaired-surrogate regex, confirms the emoji is not split before the ellipsis, and preserves the expected compact parent-context output shape.
```
diff --git a//tmp/proof-wt-96569/demo.mts b//tmp/proof-wt-96569/demo.mts
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000000000000ŌĆ”[40 ├Ś'0' elided]..0a484dea8938b2874ccb8dfbcd0e58d5e42b83c0
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/proof-wt-96569/demo.mts
@@ -0,0 +1,56 @@
+import { summarizeParentMessage } from "./extensions/msteams/src/thread-parent-context.ts";
+
+const UNPAIRED_SURROGATE_RE =
+  /[\uD800-\uDBFF](?![\uDC00-\uDFFF])|(?<![\uD800-\uDBFF])[\uDC00-\uDFFF]/;
+
+function hexCodeUnits(value: string): string {
+  return [...value]
+    .map((char) =>
+      [...char]
+        .map((_unit, index) => char.charCodeAt(index).toString(16).padStart(4, "0"))
+        .join(" "),
+    )
+    .join(" ");
+}
+
+function tailCodeUnits(value: string, count: number): string {
+  return Array.from({ length: Math.min(count, value.length) }, (_, index) =>
+    value.charCodeAt(value.length - Math.min(count, value.length) + index)
+      .toString(16)
+      .padStart(4, "0"),
+  ).join(" ");
+}
+
+const parentText = `${"a".repeat(398)}${String.fromCodePoint(0x1f99e)}${"b".repeat(50)}`;
+const legacy = `${parentText.slice(0, 399)}ŌĆ”`;
+const fixed = summarizeParentMessage({
+  id: "parent-emoji-boundary",
+  from: { user: { displayName: "Dana" } },
+  body: { content: parentText, contentType: "text" },
+});
+
+if (!fixed) {
+  throw new Error("expected parent message summary");
+}
+
+console.log(`input.length=${parentText.length}`);
+console.log(`emoji.codePoint=${String.fromCodePoint(0x1f99e).codePointAt(0)?.toString(16)}`);
+console.log("BEFORE legacy slice(0, 399) + ellipsis");
+console.log(`before.length=${legacy.length}`);
+console.log(`before.tailCodeUnits=${tailCodeUnits(legacy, 5)}`);
+console.log(`before.loneSurrogate=${UNPAIRED_SURROGATE_RE.test(legacy)}`);
+console.log(`before.loneSurrogateCodeUnit=${legacy.charCodeAt(398).toString(16)}`);
+try {
+  encodeURIComponent(legacy);
+  console.log("before.encodeURIComponent=ok");
+} catch (error) {
+  console.log(`before.encodeURIComponent=${error instanceof Error ? error.name : String(error)}`);
+}
+
+console.log("AFTER summarizeParentMessage (real msteams repair path)");
+console.log(`after.text=${fixed.text}`);
+console.log(`after.length=${fixed.text.length}`);
+console.log(`after.tailCodeUnits=${tailCodeUnits(fixed.text, 5)}`);
+console.log(`after.loneSurrogate=${UNPAIRED_SURROGATE_RE.test(fixed.text)}`);
+console.log(`after.endsWithHighSurrogateEllipsis=${fixed.text.endsWith("\ud83eŌĆ”")}`);
+console.log(`after.encodeURIComponent=${encodeURIComponent(fixed.text).slice(-18)}`);

tokens used
146,950

BEFORE legacy slice(0, 399) + ellipsis
